data.title: Canopy Growth GAAP EPS of -C$1.43 misses by C$0.88, revenue of C$65.03M misses by C$5.54M
data.description:
• Canopy Growth press release (NASDAQ:CGC): Q4 GAAP EPS of -C$1.43 misses by C$0.88.
  • Revenue of C$65.03M (-10.7% Y/Y) misses by C$5.54M.
  • Additional cost reduction initiatives identified and initiated in Q4 FY2025 are expected to deliver at least C$20 million in annualized savings over the next 12-18 months

data.title: El Pollo Loco has an activist investor on the prowl
data.description:
CapitalSpring disclosed in an SEC filing that it holds an active stake of 5.2% in El Pollo Loco Holdings (NASDAQ:LOCO).

The firm indicated that it intends to discuss the restaurant company's performance, business, strategic direction, capital structure, and prospects with management, the board of directors, other El Pollo Loco (NASDAQ:LOCO) shareholders.

Those discussions could include the potential for an extraordinary transaction involving El Pollo Loco (LOCO) and other third parties, including potential acquirers and financing source.

In connection with the discussions, CapitalSpring entered into a confidentiality agreement with El Pollo Loco (LOCO). The confidentiality agreement includes customary standstill provisions pursuant to which CapitalSpring has agreed not to take certain actions regarding the company for a period of 12 months, including limits on the acquisition of additional shares.

CapitalSpring described itself as a leading institutional investor with deep expertise in foodservice, multi-location business models and related industries. CapitalSpring has invested in more than 70 restaurant brands and over 100 founder-led businesses, primarily in the U.S. and North America

Shares of El Pollo Loco (LOCO) are down 15.8% on a year-to-date basis. Short interest on the stock stands at 4.5% of the total float. Wall Street and Seeking Alpha analysts have a consensus Buy rating on the stock.

data.title: Sanofi, Regeneron fall after late-stage data for COPD therapy
data.description:
Sanofi (NASDAQ:SNY) and Regeneron (NASDAQ:REGN) traded sharply lower in the premarket on Friday after the companies announced mixed data from a Phase 3 program for itepekimab, an antibody therapy targeting the lung disorder chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD).

Based on 52-week data from their AERIFY-1 and AERIFY-2 late-stage trials, the duo said while the former study met the primary endpoint with a statistically significant and clinically meaningful impact, the latter failed to reach the same goal.

The twin studies were designed to evaluate itepekimab as part of a combination regimen with dosing done every two weeks and four weeks against placebo in over 2,000 adults aged 40-85 years who were former smokers.

As for the primary endpoint, the itepekimab combo caused a 27% and 2% reduction in moderate or severe acute COPD exacerbations over 52 weeks in AERIFY-1 and AERIFY-2, respectively.

In terms of tolerability, the experimental therapy designed to target the COPD-linked inflammatory agent interleukin-33 indicated a consistent safety profile across both dosing regimens.

The overall rates of adverse events stood at 67% and 68% for every two weeks and every four weeks dosing regimens compared to 68% in placebo, respectively, in AERIFY-1. The trial also indicated 1% of AEs leading to death for each arm compared to 2% for placebo.

In AERIFY-2, where AEs leading to death stood at 3% for each arm compared to 2% for placebo, overall rates of AEs reached 64% and 71% for every two weeks and every four weeks dosing regimens compared to 64% for placebo, respectively.  

Sanofi (NASDAQ:SNY) and Regeneron (NASDAQ:REGN) said they are reviewing the results before starting discussions with regulators to decide on the next steps. The companies' AERIFY clinical program includes two more ongoing trials: the Phase 2 AERIFY-3 trial and a Phase 3 study named AERIFY-4.

data.title: Group Eleven Resources names Jasmine Lau as CFO
data.description:
• Group Eleven Resources (OTCPK:GRLVF) said it has appointed  Jasmine Lau as its finance chief, effective May 30.
  • Lau has over 16 years' experience in the resource sector, having served as the CFO for several mineral exploration companies.
  • She is currently the CFO of Minaurum Gold, Forte Minerals, and Cascadia Minerals.

data.title: Trump’s return sparks public debate in Germany over gold reserves held abroad
data.description:
The safety of Germany’s gold reserves held overseas and in New York in particular, until recently mainly a talking point for the country’s far-right party and gold bugs, is reportedly becoming a matter of public debate with Donald Trump back in the White House.

The Bundesbank, Germany’s central bank, has the world’s second-largest stock of gold at 3,352 tonnes, and one-third of it is stored at the Federal Reserve Bank of New York.

Those holdings have occasionally attracted scrutiny in the past, and the right-wing Alternative for Germany (AfD) has embraced calls for a return of the country’s gold back home, Reuters reported on Friday.

Trump’s clashes with longtime allies over trade and security, along with his criticism of the Federal Reserve, have recently brought the issue back into the spotlight, drawing more attention from mainstream commentators.

Germany’s Taxpayers Federation sent letters this week to the Bundesbank and the Finance Ministry calling on them to repatriate the gold stored in the United States, the report said.

“Trump wants to control the Fed, which would also mean controlling the German gold reserves in the U.S.” Michael Jaeger, the Taxpayers Federation’s vice-president, told Reuters. “It’s our money, it should be brought back.”

Markus Ferber, an influential member of the European Parliament for Germany’s ruling Christian Democrats, said the United States was “no longer the reliable partner it used to be.”

“Trump is erratic and one cannot rule out that someday he will come up with creative ideas how to treat foreign gold reserves,” he said. “The Bundesbank’s policy for gold reserves has to reflect the new geopolitical realities.”

Public broadcasters ZDF and ARD have also recently carried reports asking how safe Germany’s gold is in New York.

Any hint that Germany might be considering moving gold out of New York would be politically sensitive as it could be interpreted as lack of confidence in the Federal Reserve and its independence, the report noted.

data.title: Laurentian Bank of Canada Non-GAAP EPS of C$0.73, revenue of C$242.52M

data.title: Shoe Carnival GAAP EPS of $0.34, revenue of $277.72M
data.description:
• Shoe Carnival press release (NASDAQ:SCVL): Q1 GAAP EPS of $0.34.
  • Revenue of $277.72M (-7.5% Y/Y).
  • Comparable stores net sales declined 8.1 percent, of which the Company estimates approximately 1 percent was due to lost sales as impacted by the rebanner strategy.

  • Based on first quarter EPS exceeding market expectations, rebanner strategy momentum, and some improvement in macroeconomic uncertainties, the Company reaffirmed its entire Fiscal 2025 outlook and continues to expect the following:

    • Net Sales: $1.15 billion to $1.23 billion, representing a range of down 4 percent to up 2 percent versus Fiscal 2024.
    • GAAP EPS: $1.60 to $2.10, inclusive of the rebanner strategy's initial year costs.
    • Gross Profit Margin: 35 percent to 36 percent.
    • SG&A: $350 million to $360 million.
    • Capital Expenditures: $45 to $60 million.

data.title: Qudian reports Q1 results

  • EPADS of $0.12.

data.title: Commodity Roundup: Gold set for fifth monthly gain amid global tensions; base metals slip
data.description:
Gold prices were on course for their fifth monthly gain on Friday, buoyed by persistent economic and geopolitical tensions, while base metals eased amid fading US tariff optimism.

Spot gold (XAUUSD:CUR) was trading around $3,296.98 an ounce at the time of writing, up over 2% for the month.

The safe-haven metal, however, faced a weekly fall as investors turned cautious ahead of the US PCE inflation report that could provide fresh insights on the Federal Reserve’s interest rate trajectory.

Gold typically benefits from lower interest rates as it reduces the opportunity cost of holding the non-yielding asset.

Saxo Bank's Ole Hansen, in a recent note, stated that gold has settled into a relatively narrow, but downward-trending range as it consolidates after several months of strong performance.

"Currently, gold is lacking a clear directional trend, primarily due to conflicting macroeconomic signals and investor hesitation. Markets are waiting to assess the full economic impact of Trump’s shifting trade policies."

Most analysts, he said, expect the broader effects to be negative, which could prove supportive for gold, particularly under the looming risk of stagflation—a scenario characterized by slowing growth combined with rising inflation.

Although upcoming U.S. trade agreements—especially with major partners in Europe and Asia—could help mitigate some of the adverse economic effects and reduce demand for traditional safe-haven assets like precious metals, persistent concerns remain, Hansen noted.

"Chief among them is the widening U.S. budget deficit, which raises alarms about long-term fiscal sustainability."

In Saxo Bank's view, this, combined with stagflation risks and continued central bank demand, will likely continue to support investment interest in gold and other precious metals such as silver (XAGUSD:CUR) and platinum (XPTUSD:CUR).

Elsewhere, industrial metals declined on Friday as the dollar strengthened, and market optimism waned following a court decision to reinstate the broadest tariffs imposed by U.S. President Donald Trump.

In other London metals, aluminium (LMAHDS03:COM), zinc (LMZSDS03:COM), lead (LL1:COM), nickel (LN1:COM) and tin (LMSNDS03:COM) dipped. Copper (HG1:COM) slipped 3.7% this week.

ETFs liked to precious metals: (NYSEARCA:GLD), (GDX),(GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ),(SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

data.title: Nvidia, Dell to provide new supercomputer to US Department of Energy
data.description:
The U.S. Department of Energy said on Thursday that its new supercomputer Doudna will use technologies from Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) and Dell Technologies (NYSE:DELL).

The U.S. Secretary of Energy Chris Wright announced a new contract with Dell to develop NERSC-10, the next flagship supercomputer at the National Energy Research Scientific Computing Center, or NERSC, a DOE user facility at the Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory.

The new system, due in 2026, will be named after Jennifer Doudna, the Berkeley Lab-based biochemist who was awarded the 2020 Nobel Prize for Chemistry for her work on gene-editing technology CRISPR.

The DOE said the Dell system will be powered by Nvidia's next-generation Vera Rubin platform and will help in large-scale high-performance computing, or HPC, workloads like those in molecular dynamics, high-energy physics, and AI training and inference.

“Doudna is a time machine for science — compressing years of discovery into days,” said Nvidia Founder and CEO Jensen Huang.

The DOE noted that Doudna will be one of the most advanced supercomputers ever deployed by it.

"It will be a powerhouse for rapid innovation that will transform our efforts to develop abundant, affordable energy supplies and advance breakthroughs in quantum computing. AI is the Manhattan Project of our time, and Doudna will help ensure America’s scientists have the tools they need to win the global race for AI dominance," said Wright.

NERSC Director Sudip Dosanjh noted that Doudna will be connected to DOE experimental facilities via the Energy Sciences Network, or ESnet, allowing scientists to stream data into the system from across the country and analyze it in near-real time.
